page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a large 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix math. The instance is equipped with 248 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.75 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the c4-highmem-32 demonstrates strong performance in single-threaded operations, including single-core Geekbench scores and single-threaded gzip compression. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 and Passmark CPU physics testing also rank in the strong tier. The hardware shows average performance in raw multi-core stress-ng tests and memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads measuring 144.63 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the onboard AMX instructions enable top-tier text generation speeds of 421.19 tokens/sec for small 135M LLM models, though performance drops significantly to a poor rating of 2.38 tokens/sec when attempting text generation on large 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-32 offers a high density of dedicated compute resources and RAM without the added expense of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a highly viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require persistent local NVMe drives. The instance is well-suited for high-throughput static web serving, memory-bound database operations, text processing, and medium-scale machine learning inference tasks that can leverage CPU-based matrix acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 248 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. With 16 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and support for Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, this instance delivers strong single-core performance and efficient matrix math capabilities. It does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks highlight strong capabilities in static web serving, single-threaded gzip compression, and small-model LLM inference, while multi-core stress tests and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB makes this server highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, text processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-32 features 32 dedicated vCPUs, 248 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized, dedicated compute instance featuring advanced matrix extensions for accelerated CPU-based workloads and high-density memory operations.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
248 GB of system memory providing 7.75 GB of RAM per vCPU
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong performance in static web serving and single-threaded compression
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for memory-bound databases, text processing, and medium-scale CPU inference
